public_goals: Understand the real expense picture before agreeing to reimbursement expectations.
Avoid saying yes just because the paperwork and accounts have always been controlled by the other parent.
Get a fair next step that improves clarity without another escalation.
private_concerns: Feels embarrassed about not understanding the expense records confidently.
Worries disagreement will be interpreted as incompetence rather than caution.
Fears being pushed into a commitment simply because the other parent sounds more informed.
red_lines: Will not agree to reimbursement expectations they cannot independently understand.
Will not pretend confidence just to keep the session calm.
emotional_triggers: being told something is obvious when they do not understand it
pressure to decide before documentation is shared
disclosure_tendencies: admits uncertainty only after explicit reassurance
may downplay confusion to avoid looking dependent
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: becomes quieter and more deferential if the process seems to favor the more informed parent
re-engages when the process explicitly protects informed participation
starting_positions: Primary position: I need to see and understand the records before any reimbursement expectation is treated as fair.
Secondary position: a shared written summary process would be safer than trying to settle the numbers verbally.
likely_openings: I do not want to agree just because you have always handled the paperwork.
I need to understand what these expenses really are before I say yes to any of it.
